data.title: OTC Markets Group introduces Cannara Biotech Inc. to OTCQX, a producer of premium cannabis products.Important Numbers: 12,000 securities, TSX-V: LOVE.V, OTCQX: LOVFF FSX: 8CB.
data.description: On January 14, 2026, OTC Markets Group Inc. disclosed that Cannara Biotech Inc., a premium cannabis producer, has met the requirements to trade on the OTCQX® Best Market. Operating regulated markets for 12,000 U.S. and international securities, OTC Markets Group Inc. facilitates this transition for Cannara Biotech Inc. from the OTCQB® Venture Market. Cannara Biotech Inc. can now trade under the tickers TSX-V: LOVE.V, OTCQX: LOVFF FSX: 8CB on the OTCQX® Best Market, expanding its reach to a broader investor base.

data.title: Inflation calms, setting stage for Fed rate cut in September. CPI data released on August 14, 2024. By: Lawrence Yun.
data.description: Recent data released shows that the Consumer Price Index (CPI) as of August 14, 2024, indicates a more stable inflation rate. This development has positioned the Federal Reserve to initiate a series of interest rate cuts starting in September. Economist Lawrence Yun commented on this promising trend.

data.title: Domestic Metals engages ICP Securities for market making at C$7,500/month for 4 months, renewal options, no performance factors.
data.description: Domestic Metals Corp. has partnered with ICP Securities Inc. for market making services, using ICP's algorithm, ICP Premium™. The agreement is for a monthly fee of C$7,500, plus taxes, starting from Jan. 23, 2026, for an initial 4-month term, with automatic renewal for subsequent 1-month terms unless either party provides 30 days notice. The agreement does not include performance factors or stock options, and ICP and its clients may acquire an interest in the company's securities in the future.

data.title: Jamie Dimon rules out Fed Chair role, but open to Treasury Secretary if asked by president. Prefers being own boss. Full story on Benzinga.com.
data.description: JPMorgan Chase & Co.'s CEO, Jamie Dimon, stated he has no interest in becoming the Chairman of the Federal Reserve but is open to taking on the role of Secretary of the Treasury if offered by the President. Dimon firmly ruled out leading the Federal Reserve, even if approached by a U.S. President. However, he expressed willingness to serve as Treasury Secretary, emphasizing the importance of considering such requests from the President. Dimon's position was disclosed in an interview with Bloomberg's David Rubenstein, where he indicated a "positively no chance" of assuming the Fed leadership but potentially considering a senior Cabinet position. For more details, visit the complete article on Benzinga.com.
